source.title: MedlinePlus: Heart attack
source.note: Synthetic vignette inspired by public symptom descriptions; not patient data.

input.chief_complaint: Crushing chest pain with sweating and nausea for 30 minutes
input.history: History of diabetes and hypertension. Pain radiates to left arm.

rationale: Chest pain pattern with hemodynamic instability (SBP<90 and HR>130) should be treated as critical until proven otherwise.

source.title: MedlinePlus: Pulmonary embolism
source.note: Synthetic vignette inspired by public symptom descriptions; not patient data.

input.chief_complaint: Sudden shortness of breath and sharp chest pain when breathing in
input.history: Recent long-haul flight. No prior lung disease.

rationale: Acute dyspnea with hypoxemia (SpO₂<92%) plus cardiopulmonary complaint requires immediate escalation.

source.title: CDC: Stroke signs and symptoms
source.note: Synthetic vignette inspired by public stroke warning signs; not patient data.

input.chief_complaint: Sudden slurred speech and weakness one side started 25 minutes ago
input.history: History of hypertension. Last known well 25 minutes ago.

rationale: Focal neurologic deficit within hours suggests possible stroke and warrants urgent pathway activation.

source.title: NHS: Sudden severe headache (thunderclap)
source.note: Synthetic vignette inspired by public red-flag headache guidance; not patient data.

input.chief_complaint: Worst headache of my life, sudden onset during exercise
input.history: No prior migraine history. Vomited once. Photophobia.

rationale: Thunderclap headache is a neurologic red flag requiring urgent evaluation for intracranial pathology.

source.title: CDC: Sepsis information
source.note: Synthetic vignette inspired by public sepsis warning signs; not patient data.

input.chief_complaint: Confusion and shaking chills
input.history: Family reports sudden change in mental status today. Fever since yesterday.

rationale: Altered mental status with very high fever is consistent with severe infection risk and requires immediate escalation.

source.title: CDC: Meningitis symptoms
source.note: Synthetic vignette inspired by public symptom descriptions; not patient data.

input.chief_complaint: Severe headache, fever, and confusion
input.history: Neck stiffness. Photophobia. Symptoms started this morning.

rationale: High fever (>=39.5°C) plus confusion is a critical red-flag pattern for CNS infection or other emergencies.

source.title: Mayo Clinic: Fainting (syncope)
source.note: Synthetic vignette inspired by public syncope guidance; not patient data.

input.chief_complaint: Fainting while walking up stairs
input.history: Brief loss of consciousness, now awake. Felt lightheaded beforehand.

rationale: Syncope/near-syncope is a red flag that typically requires urgent evaluation for cardiac or other causes.

source.title: MedlinePlus: Vomiting blood
source.note: Synthetic vignette inspired by public symptom descriptions; not patient data.

input.chief_complaint: Vomiting blood and dizziness
input.history: Takes NSAIDs most days. Nearly fainted when standing.

rationale: Hematemesis with hypotension/tachycardia suggests significant GI bleed with hemodynamic instability.

source.title: MedlinePlus: Black or tarry stools
source.note: Synthetic vignette inspired by public symptom descriptions; not patient data.

input.chief_complaint: Black tarry stools and weakness today
input.history: Lightheaded when standing. No vomiting blood.

rationale: Melena suggests GI bleed; even with borderline vitals it warrants urgent escalation for evaluation.

source.title: MedlinePlus: Vaginal bleeding during pregnancy
source.note: Synthetic vignette inspired by public guidance; not patient data.

input.history: 8 weeks pregnant. Bleeding started 2 hours ago and worsening.

rationale: Pregnancy bleeding is an obstetric red flag; urgent evaluation is needed to rule out emergencies.

source.title: WHO: Postpartum haemorrhage (overview)
source.note: Synthetic vignette inspired by postpartum hemorrhage risk; not patient data.

input.chief_complaint: Heavy vaginal bleeding 1 hour after delivery
input.history: Soaking pads rapidly; feels faint.

rationale: Severe bleeding with hypotension/tachycardia indicates hemodynamic instability and obstetric emergency.

source.title: MedlinePlus: Anaphylaxis

input.history: Known food allergy. Wheezing and lightheadedness.

source.title: CDC: Sepsis information

input.history: No focal symptoms. Started last night and worse today.
